=== Quick Fix Inventory ===

[D1] (10:59-10:65) TS377003: Missing errors Boom in the expected Effect type. effect(missingEffectError)
  Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Catch all errors with Effect.catch"
  Fix 3: "Catch unexpected errors with Effect.catchTag"

=== Quick Fix Application Results ===

=== [D1] Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 2: "Catch all errors with Effect.catch" ===

--- file:///.src/missingEffectError_preview.ts ---
// @effect-diagnostics *:off
// @effect-diagnostics missingEffectError:warning
import type * as Effect from "effect/Effect"
import { Data } from "effect"

class Boom extends Data.TaggedError("Boom")<{}> {}
declare const effect: Effect.Effect<number, Boom>

// @ts-expect-error
export const preview: () => Effect.Effect<number> = () => Effect.catch(effect, () => Effect.dieMessage("TODO: catch not implemented"))


=== [D1] Fix 3: "Catch unexpected errors with Effect.catchTag" ===

--- file:///.src/missingEffectError_preview.ts ---
// @effect-diagnostics *:off
// @effect-diagnostics missingEffectError:warning
import type * as Effect from "effect/Effect"
import { Data } from "effect"

class Boom extends Data.TaggedError("Boom")<{}> {}
declare const effect: Effect.Effect<number, Boom>

// @ts-expect-error
export const preview: () => Effect.Effect<number> = () => Effect.catchTags(effect, { "Boom": () => Effect.dieMessage("TODO: catchTags() not implemented for Boom") })
